desc: Command: Matches pipe centerlines by shifting the second pipe in X/Y.
summary: Command: Matches pipe centerlines by shifting the second pipe in X/Y. Delegates alignment logic to MatchPipeCenters. Uses GetMEPCurve indirectly via the logic class. Related: MEPSystemFilletCommand for full fillet, MEPSystemAutoFilletCommand for auto-detect elbow/tee. WORKFLOW: 1. User selects reference pipe (stays in place) 2. User selects target pipe (will be moved) 3. Target pipe is shifted in X/Y to align with reference pipe centerline 4. Pipes can now be connected with a fitting
usecase: Aligning branch pipes to main pipes before connecting. Fixing misaligned pipes that can't accept fittings. Preparing pipes for automatic connection with FilletMEPCurve.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least two pipes must exist in the model.
postcondition: Target pipe centerline aligns with reference pipe extension. Pipes are now in position to be connected with an elbow.
sideeffects: Moves the target pipe element horizontally.
logictree: check: document.isOpen → continueaction: promptUserToSelectReferencePipe → getReferencePipeaction: promptUserToSelectTargetPipe → getTargetPipecheck: bothPipesValid → continue, else → erroraction: calculateXYOffset → offsetaction: moveTargetPipe → shiftresolve: success → complete, else → showError

selection_inputs.name: ReferencePipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select REFERENCE pipe (this pipe stays in place)

selection_inputs.name: TargetPipe
selection_inputs.prompt: Select TARGET pipe (this pipe will be shifted)

thinkingsteps: Calculating X/Y offset to align centerlines...
successprompts: Pipes aligned. Shifted by X={XOffset}, Y={YOffset}.
Centerlines matched — pipes ready for fitting.
